briefdescription.para: For all objects deriving from Handle (including Actors). @SINCE_1_0.0.
Start index for Actor. @SINCE_1_0.0.
Actor range: 0 to 9999 @SINCE_1_0.0.
Property start index for classes deriving directly from Actor. @SINCE_1_0.0.
Second-level and onwards derived objects should increment their start index by this. @SINCE_1_0.0.
Used by PanGestureDetector. @SINCE_1_0.0.
Start index for Renderer. @SINCE_1_1.44.
Renderer range: 9,000,000 to 9,009,999 @SINCE_2_1.13.
The index when registering a property should start from this number. @SINCE_1_0.0.
Default Property Range: 0 to 9999999 @SINCE_1_0.0.
The maximum index supported when registering a property @SINCE_1_0.0.
The index when registering an animatable property should start from this number. (SceneGraph properties per type) @SINCE_1_0.0.
The index when registering an animatable property without uniform mapping should start from this number. (SceneGraph properties per type) @SINCE_2_4.15.
The maximum index supported when registering an animatable property without uniform mapping @SINCE_2_4.15.
The maximum index supported when registering an animatable property @SINCE_1_0.0.
The index when registering a child property should start from this number. (Event side properties per instance) @SINCE_1_1.35.
The maximum index supported when registering a child property @SINCE_1_1.35.
The index at which custom properties start (SceneGraph and Event side properties per instance) @SINCE_1_0.0.
The maximum index supported for custom properties @SINCE_1_1.45.
The maximum index that Core properties can go up to @SINCE_1_1.45.
Enumeration for the property index ranges.

detaileddescription.para: Enumerations are being used here rather than static constants so that switch statements can be used to compare property indices. @SINCE_1_0.0

briefdescription.para: Signal type for Dali::PropertyNotification::NotifySignal(). @SINCE_1_0.0.
Extracts a pointer to the held type of an Any object from a pointer to that Any object (NULL if empty).

detaileddescription.para: AnyCast helper functions

@SINCE_1_0.0
any
Pointer to an Any object
Pointer to the Type held

any
const Pointer to an Any object
const Pointer to the Type held

any
The reference to an Any object
Type value of type Type

briefdescription.para: Extracts a reference to the held value of type Type from an Any object from a reference to that Any object.

any
The reference to an Any object
A reference to the Type value of type Type

briefdescription.para: Extracts a const reference to the held value of type Type from an Any object from a const reference to that Any object.

@SINCE_1_0.0
any
The reference to an Any object

briefdescription.para: Template wrapper to downcast a base object handle to derived class handle.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
handle
Handle to a base object
Handle pointer to either a valid deriving handle or an uninitialized handle
The BaseHandle has been initialized.

briefdescription.para: Equality operator. @SINCE_1_0.0.

simplesect.para: True if the handle handles point to the same Dali resource, or if both are NULL

briefdescription.para: Equality operator. @SINCE_1_0.0.

simplesect.para: True if the handle handles point to the different Dali resources

briefdescription.para: Less than operator. @SINCE_1_0.0.

simplesect.para: True if lhs less than rhs

briefdescription.para: Gets the implementation of a handle.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
handle
The handle
A reference to the object the handle points at

briefdescription.para: Gets the implementation of a handle.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
handle
The handle
A reference to the object the handle points at

briefdescription.para: LessThanCondition compares whether property is less than arg.

detaileddescription.para:
property type: bool (false = 0.0f, true = 1.0f) float (float) vector2 (the 2D length) vector3 (the 3D length) vector4 (the 4D length) @SINCE_1_0.0
arg
The argument for the condition
A property condition function object

briefdescription.para: GreaterThanCondition compares whether property is greater than arg.

detaileddescription.para:
property type: bool (false = 0.0f, true = 1.0f) float (float) vector2 (the 2D length) vector3 (the 3D length) vector4 (the 4D length) @SINCE_1_0.0
arg
The argument for the condition
A property condition function object

briefdescription.para: InsideCondition compares whether property is greater than arg0 and less than arg1.

detaileddescription.para:
property type: bool (false = 0.0f, true = 1.0f) float (float) vector2 (the 2D length) vector3 (the 3D length) vector4 (the 4D length) @SINCE_1_0.0
arg0
The first argument for the condition
arg1
The second argument for the condition
A property condition function object

briefdescription.para: OutsideCondition compares whether property is less than arg0 or greater than arg1.

detaileddescription.para:
property type: bool (false = 0.0f, true = 1.0f) float (float) vector2 (the 2D length) vector3 (the 3D length) vector4 (the 4D length) @SINCE_1_0.0
arg0
The first argument for the condition
arg1
The second argument for the condition
A property condition function object

briefdescription.para: Detects when a property changes by stepAmount from initialValue, in both positive and negative directions. This will continue checking for multiples of stepAmount.

detaileddescription.para:
property type: float (float) vector2 (the 2D length) vector3 (the 3D length) vector4 (the 4D length) quaternion (the 3D length of eular angle) @SINCE_1_0.0
stepAmount
The step size required to trigger condition
initialValue
The initial value to step from
A property condition function object

briefdescription.para: Receives notifications as a property goes above/below the inputted values. Values must be ordered and can be either ascending or descending.

detaileddescription.para:
property type: float (float) vector2 (the 2D length) vector3 (the 3D length) vector4 (the 4D length) @SINCE_1_0.0
steps
List of values to receive notifications for as a property crosses them
A property condition function object

briefdescription.para: Converts the value of the property into a string and appends to an output stream.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
ouputStream
The output stream operator
value
The value to insert
The output stream operator

briefdescription.para: Converts the key/value pairs of the property map into a string and append to an output stream.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_1.28
stream
The output stream operator
map
The map to insert
The output stream operator

briefdescription.para: Converts the values of the property array into a string and append to an output stream.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_1.28
stream
The output stream operator
array
The array to insert
The output stream operator

briefdescription.para: Convert the key into a string and append to an output stream.

@SINCE_1_2.7
stream
The output stream operator.
key
the key to convert
The output stream operator.
